codeReview.summary: Implementation provides SimpleReranker (keyword-based) as MVP fallback but has two blocking bugs from prior review that persist: (1) RerankableCandidates trait is dead code creating empty MemoryEntry objects, (2) BM25 stream generates random UUIDs breaking RRF fusion.

issues.description: RerankableCandidates trait impl creates MemoryEntry with empty fields (label: String::new(), content: String::new(), repository: String::new()) but is never called anywhere. TripleStreamService::build_rerank_candidates (line 697-711 in triple_stream.rs) fetches entries directly from memory_store.get() instead. The trait is dead code that should be removed or properly integrated.

BM25 search stream generates random UUIDs with Uuid::new_v4() instead of using actual memory entry IDs. This means the same memory entry gets different UUIDs on each call, making RRF fusion impossible since IDs won't match across streams. Comment even says 'Placeholder' and 'In real implementation, recall results would link to memory entries'.

Feature description says 'BGE-reranker for cross-encoder reranking' but only SimpleReranker (keyword-based) is implemented. Bge reranker type is defined but never actually implemented. This is acceptable for MVP but should be documented.

sharedStateObservations.observation: RerankableCandidates trait is exported from lib.rs (line 14) but is never used in the codebase. This is dead code that clutters the API.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: grep shows only definition/export, no actual usage of to_rerank_candidates() anywhere

sharedStateObservations.observation: Magic numbers in SimpleReranker scoring algorithm (label_score * 0.2 + content_score * 0.3 + phrase_score * 0.3 + density_score * 0.2) are hardcoded but not configurable. These weights should be in CrossEncoderConfig.

summary: FAIL - Two blocking bugs persist from prior review: (1) RerankableCandidates trait at line 518 creates MemoryEntry with empty fields but is never called - the TripleStreamService builds candidates differently. (2) BM25 UUID bug at line 777 generates random UUIDs breaking cross-stream fusion. Both issues remain unfixed.
